"cvss_severities": {"cvss_v3": {"score": 4.3, "vector_string": "CVSS:3.1/AV:N/AC:L/PR:N/UI:R/S:U/C:N/I:L/A:N"}, "cvss_v4": {"score": 0.0, "vector_string": null}}, "cwes": [{"cwe_id": "CWE-113", "name": "Improper Neutralization of CRLF Sequences in HTTP Headers ('HTTP Request/Response Splitting')"}, {"cwe_id": "CWE-1287", "name": "Improper Validation of Specified Type of Input"}], "description": "### Summary

The `serialize()` function in `hono/cookie` validates `domain` and `path` options against characters that corrupt `Set-Cookie` header syntax (`;`, `\r`, `\n`), but does not apply the same validation to `sameSite` and `priority`. An application that passes user-controlled input into either option may produce a `Set-Cookie` response header containing attacker-chosen additional attributes.

### Details

When constructing a `Set-Cookie` header value, `serialize()` appends the `sameSite` and `priority` option values directly into the output string after a presentation-only transformation (capitalizing the first character). Although the TypeScript type signature constrains these options to specific string literals, that constraint is not enforced at runtime; any string value, including one containing `;` or line-feed characters, passes through unchanged.

The validation guard that rejects `;`, `\r`, and `\n` from `domain` and `path` is not applied to `sameSite` or `priority`. An application that passes a request-derived value to either option therefore provides an injection point into the header line.

This issue arises when an application passes user-controlled input to the `sameSite` or `priority` option of `setCookie()` or `serialize()`.

### Impact

An attacker who can control the `sameSite` or `priority` option value may inject additional attributes into a `Set-Cookie` response header.

This may lead to:

- Cookie attribute injection — overriding `Domain`, `Path`, `HttpOnly`, `Secure`, or `Max-Age` for the affected cookie
- HTTP response header injection on runtimes that do not strictly validate header values, enabling a second attacker-controlled `Set-Cookie` header in the same response

This issue affects applications that pass user-derived input into the `sameSite` or `priority` option of `hono/cookie` serialization functions.", "epss": {"percentage": 0.00216, "percentile": 0.11872}, "ghsa_id": "GHSA-3hrh-pfw6-9m5x", "github_reviewed_at": "2026-06-04T17:59:25Z", "html_url": "https://github.com/advisories/GHSA-3hrh-pfw6-9m5x", "identifiers": [{"type": "GHSA", "value": "GHSA-3hrh-pfw6-9m5x"}, {"type": "CVE", "value": "CVE-2026-47675"}], "nvd_published_at": "2026-05-28T17:16:32Z", "published_at": "2026-06-04T17:59:25Z", "references": ["https://github.com/honojs/hono/security/advisories/GHSA-3hrh-pfw6-9m5x", "https://nvd.nist.gov/vuln/detail/CVE-2026-47675", "https://github.com/honojs/hono/commit/905aedbc20661e0e2fa378783a7ec44a5c3df43d", "https://github.com/honojs/hono/releases/tag/v4.12.21", "https://github.com/advisories/GHSA-3hrh-pfw6-9m5x"], "repository_advisory_url": "https://api.github.com/repos/honojs/hono/security-advisories/GHSA-3hrh-pfw6-9m5x", "severity": "medium", "source_code_location": "https://github.com/honojs/hono", "summary": "Hono: Cookie helper does not sanitize sameSite and priority, allowing Set-Cookie injection", "type": "reviewed", "updated_at": "2026-06-04T17:59:27Z", "url": "https://api.github.com/advisories/GHSA-3hrh-pfw6-9m5x", "vulnerabilities": [{"first_patched_version": "4.12.21", "package": {"ecosystem": "npm", "name": "hono"}, "vulnerable_functions": [], "vulnerable_version_range": "< 4.12.21"}], "withdrawn_at": null}

title: Hono: Cookie helper does not sanitize sameSite and priority, allowing Set-Cookie injection
